name: Tooltip
description: Tooltips reveal a short text label on hover or focus — naming an icon-only button, or showing a value that's been truncated in a dense table. They're especially valuable on desktop, where hover exists. The karoo-ui wrapper defaults `arrow` to true per Cartrack's design guidelines (real MUI default is false); every other prop is untouched.
keyRule: Use a tooltip for brief, supplementary text — an icon's name, a truncated cell's full value. Never put essential information or actions only in a tooltip.

variants.name: Plain
variants.whenToUse: A short `title` naming or clarifying the trigger `children`.

variants.name: Truncation reveal (usage pattern)
variants.whenToUse: Wrap a truncated/clipped text element as `children` and pass its full value as `title` — there is no dedicated variant, just this usage.

anatomy.name: children
anatomy.description: the single React element being described (icon button, truncated text) — must accept a ref and forward extra props.

anatomy.name: Popper / tooltip surface
anatomy.description: the small dark floating surface, positioned via Popper (`placement` prop).

anatomy.name: title
anatomy.description: concise text/node (ideally a few words) shown inside the surface.

anatomy.name: arrow
anatomy.description: optional pointer connecting the surface to the trigger — defaulted to visible by the karoo-ui wrapper.

behaviors.name: Trigger
behaviors.description: Appears on hover and on keyboard focus by default; dismisses on blur/mouse-out or Escape. Disable individually via `disableHoverListener` / `disableFocusListener` / `disableTouchListener`.

behaviors.name: Delay
behaviors.description: `enterDelay` (real MUI default 100ms) and `leaveDelay` (default 0ms) control show/hide timing; `enterNextDelay` shortens the delay for consecutive tooltips.

behaviors.name: Positioning
behaviors.description: `placement` (default 'bottom') flips to stay within the viewport via the underlying Popper; never covers its own trigger.

states.treatment: Not rendered until triggered (or controlled via `open`).
Dark surface, light text, near the trigger, with an arrow by default (Cartrack override).

specs.value: grey 700 @ ~92% opacity (MUI's real unmodified default), white text — passes AA
Roboto ~11px (theme.typography.pxToRem(11), which resolves to 11px given this app's 14px htmlFontSize override)
100ms (real MUI default, not overridden by karoo-ui)

props.name: title
props.description: Concise tooltip text (ideally a few words). Zero-length/undefined/null/false are never displayed.

props.name: children
props.description: The single trigger element being described; it must forward a ref and spread extra props (icon button, span wrapping truncated text, etc.).

props.name: arrow
props.description: Shows a pointer arrow. Real MUI default is false; the karoo-ui wrapper defaults this to true.

props.name: open
props.description: Controlled open state. Pair with onOpen/onClose.

props.name: onOpen
props.description: Callback fired when the tooltip requests to open.

props.name: onClose
props.description: Callback fired when the tooltip requests to close.

props.name: disableHoverListener
props.description: Do not respond to hover events. Default false.

props.name: disableFocusListener
props.description: Do not respond to focus-visible events. Default false.

props.name: disableInteractive
props.description: Closes when the pointer moves onto the tooltip surface before leaveDelay expires. Default false.

props.name: followCursor
props.description: Tooltip follows the cursor over the wrapped element. Default false.

doThis: Label every icon-only button with a tooltip (via IconButton + Tooltip).
Keep title text to a few words.
Rely on the default focus + hover triggers — don't disable both.
dontDoThis: Don't put actions or essential info only in a tooltip.
Don't rely on hover alone (touch/keyboard need the default focus/touch listeners left enabled).
accessibility: Tooltip associates itself with its trigger via aria-describedby (or aria-labelledby if `describeChild` is false, the default) automatically.
Shows on focus as well as hover by default, and Escape dismisses it.
The trigger still needs its own accessible name if it's icon-only (e.g. aria-label on an IconButton) — the tooltip title alone isn't always sufficient for all assistive tech paths.
